title: NET_L2_MULTICAST_SKIP_JOIN_SOLICIT_NODE requires LoRa broadcast knowledge
description:
lichen_l2.c lines 459-476 return NET_L2_MULTICAST | NET_L2_MULTICAST_SKIP_JOIN_SOLICIT_NODE:

```c
/*
 * NET_L2_MULTICAST: Tells Zephyr this L2 can deliver IP multicast frames.
 * LoRa is inherently broadcast - all transmissions reach all receivers,
 * so multicast delivery works by default.
 *
 * NET_L2_MULTICAST_SKIP_JOIN_SOLICIT_NODE: Skip joining solicited-node
 * multicast groups (ff02::1:ffXX:XXXX). In a broadcast medium, all nodes
 * receive all Neighbor Solicitation messages regardless of multicast group
 * membership, making solicited-node optimization pointless. Skipping the
 * join avoids MLD report traffic that would waste precious LoRa airtime.
 * OpenThread uses the same pattern for similar reasons.
 */
```

This comment is well-written but assumes the reader knows:
1. What solicited-node multicast groups are
2. What MLD is
3. Why LoRa airtime is 'precious'

What goes wrong: A reader without IPv6 NDP knowledge cannot evaluate if the flag is correct.

Improvement: Add a one-line summary before the detail: 'LoRa is broadcast, so multicast filtering happens in software not hardware; we skip multicast group join protocol traffic since all nodes receive all frames anyway.'
